canonical_definition: Within the provided literature, news headline generation is defined as the task of creating a concise title for a news article. The sole paper discussing this behavior, "ExpandR: Teaching Dense Retrievers Beyond Queries withLLMGuidance", operationalizes it as an instruction given to an LLM. In this context, it serves as an "alternative content format" and is contrasted with another generation task, producing a tweet. The paper employs this behavior specifically within an ablation study to "evaluate the effect of an alternate content choice".
papers: Case-Based Decision-Theoretic Decoding with Quality Memories
ExpandR: Teaching Dense Retrievers Beyond Queries withLLMGuidance
Uncovering the Bigger Picture: Comprehensive Event Understanding Via Diverse News Retrieval

definitions.text: The task of creating a concise title for a news article, used as an alternative content format in an ablation study.
definitions.paper: ExpandR: Teaching Dense Retrievers Beyond Queries withLLMGuidance
